---
title: "Geographical distribution of sorghum accesions used in this study"
author: "subhash"
date: "2025-04-28"
output: html_document
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = TRUE)
```

```{r}
library(tidyverse)
library(readxl)
library(maps)

data <- read.csv("/Users/subhashmahamkali/Downloads/tpj15853-sup-0001-files1 (1).csv")
data = data[,c(1,2,5)]
colnames(data) <- c("Pinumber", "cluster","Country")
country_counts <- data %>%count(Country, name = "n")
world <- map_data("world")

map_df <- world %>% 
  group_by(region) %>% 
  summarise(long = mean(range(long)), lat = mean(range(lat))) %>%
  left_join(country_counts, by = c("region" = "Country")) %>%
  filter(!is.na(n))

#ggplot(world, aes(x = long, y = lat)) +
  #geom_polygon(aes(group = group), fill = "white", color = "gray80") +
  #geom_point(data = map_df, aes(x = long, y = lat, size = n), color = "red") +
  #theme_minimal() +
  #labs(x = "Longitude", y = "Latitude", size = "Number of Accessions") +
  #theme(panel.background = element_rect(fill = "lightblue"))

cluster_names <- c("1" = "durra",
                   "2" = "kafir",
                   "3" = "caudatum",
                   "4" = "milo/durra-bicolor",
                   "5" = "mixed/bicolor",
                   "6" = "guinea")  # change if needed

data <- data %>%
  filter(!is.na(Country)) %>%            # Remove missing countries
  mutate(cluster = as.character(cluster),
         ClusterName = cluster_names[cluster])  # Assign descriptive names

country_cluster_counts <- data %>%
  count(Country, ClusterName)

world <- map_data("world")

centroids <- world %>%
  group_by(region) %>%
  summarise(long = mean(range(long)), lat = mean(range(lat)))

map_df <- country_cluster_counts %>%
  left_join(centroids, by = c("Country" = "region")) %>%
  filter(!is.na(long))  # Keep only countries that matched map data


ggplot(world, aes(long, lat)) +
  geom_polygon(aes(group = group), fill = "white", color = "gray70") +
  geom_point(data = map_df, aes(x = long, y = lat, color = ClusterName), alpha = 0.8) +
  scale_color_manual(values = c("durra" = "green",
                                "kafir" = "orange",
                                "caudatum" = "purple",
                                "milo/durra-bicolor" = "gold",
                                "mixed/bicolor" = "brown",
                                "guinea" = "red")) +
  theme_minimal() +
  labs(x = "Longitude", y = "Latitude", size = "Accessions", color = "Cluster") +
  theme(legend.position = "right")
        #panel.background = element_rect(fill = "lightpink"))
```

```{r}
library(tidyverse)
library(readxl)
library(maps)

# Read and clean data
data2 <- read_excel("/Users/subhashmahamkali/Downloads/13068_2021_2016_MOESM1_ESM (1).xlsx")
colnames(data2) <- data2[1, ]
data2 <- data2[-1, ]
data2 = data2[, c(1:2, 5:6)]


data2$Type[data2$Type == "Weedy"] <- "Wild sorghum"
data2$Type[data2$Type == "S.propinquum"] <- "Wild sorghum"
#data2 <- data2[data2$Type != "S.propinquum", ]
data2 = data2[, c(2:4)]
colnames(data2) <- c("Pinumber", "Country", "cluster")

# Count the number of accessions for each country and cluster
country_cluster_counts <- data2 %>%
  count(Country, cluster)

# Get world map data
world <- map_data("world")
centroids <- world %>%
  group_by(region) %>%
  summarise(long = mean(range(long)), lat = mean(range(lat)))

# Merge data with map centroids
map_df <- country_cluster_counts %>%
  left_join(centroids, by = c("Country" = "region")) %>%
  filter(!is.na(long))  # Keep only countries that matched map data

# Plot the map with original cluster names
ggplot(world, aes(long, lat)) +
  geom_polygon(aes(group = group), fill = "white", color = "gray70") +
  geom_point(data = map_df, aes(x = long, y = lat, color = cluster), alpha = 0.8) +
  scale_color_manual(values = c("Improved" = "green", 
                                "Wild sorghum" = "orange", 
                                "Landrace" = "blue")) +
  theme_minimal() +
  labs(x = "Longitude", y = "Latitude", size = "Accessions", color = "Cluster") +
  theme(legend.position = "right")
```



```{r}
library(tidyverse)
library(readxl)
library(maps)
library(ggplot2)
# Read and clean the first dataset
data <- read.csv("/Users/subhashmahamkali/Downloads/tpj15853-sup-0001-files1 (1).csv")
data = data[, c(1, 2, 5)]
colnames(data) <- c("Pinumber", "cluster", "Country")
cluster_names <- c("1" = "durra", "2" = "kafir", "3" = "caudatum", "4" = "milo/durra-bicolor", 
                   "5" = "mixed/bicolor", "6" = "guinea")

# Clean the Country names in the first dataset to match world map region names
data <- data %>%
  mutate(Country = recode(Country,
                          "S. Africa" = "South Africa",
                          "USA" = "United States",
                          "Prob. China" = "China",
                          "Guatemala" = "Guatemala",
                          "Unknown" = NA_character_,  # Handle unknown cases
                          "?" = NA_character_)) %>%
  filter(!is.na(Country)) %>%
  mutate(cluster = as.character(cluster),
         ClusterName = cluster_names[cluster],
         shape = "circle")  # Assign circle shape for plot1

# Read and clean the second dataset
data2 <- read_excel("/Users/subhashmahamkali/Downloads/13068_2021_2016_MOESM1_ESM (1).xlsx")
colnames(data2) <- data2[1, ]
data2 <- data2[-1, ]
data2 = data2[, c(1:2, 5:6)]
data2$Type[data2$Type == "Weedy"] <- "Wild sorghum"
data2 <- data2[data2$Type != "S.propinquum", ]
data2 = data2[, c(2:4)]
colnames(data2) <- c("Pinumber", "Country", "cluster")

# Clean the Country names in the second dataset to match world map region names
data2 <- data2 %>%
  mutate(Country = recode(Country,
                          "S. Africa" = "South Africa",
                          "USA" = "United States",
                          "Prob. China" = "China",
                          "Guatemala" = "Guatemala",
                          "Unknown" = NA_character_,
                          "?" = NA_character_)) %>%
  filter(!is.na(Country)) %>%
  mutate(shape = "triangle")  # Default shape for plot2 as triangle

# Map for clusters from both datasets
cluster_names <- c(
  "1" = "durra", 
  "2" = "kafir", 
  "3" = "caudatum", 
  "4" = "milo/durra-bicolor", 
  "5" = "mixed/bicolor", 
  "6" = "guinea",
  "Improved" = "Improved",        # Add the Improved cluster
  "Wild sorghum" = "Wild sorghum", # Add the Wild sorghum cluster
  "Landrace" = "Landrace"         # Add the Landrace cluster
)

# Update: Assign new cluster names for 'Improved', 'Wild sorghum', and 'Landrace'
data2 <- data2 %>%
  mutate(ClusterName = case_when(
    cluster == "Improved" ~ "Improved",    # For 'Improved'
    cluster == "Wild sorghum" ~ "Wild sorghum",  # For 'Wild sorghum'
    cluster == "Landrace" ~ "Landrace",    # For 'Landrace'
    TRUE ~ cluster_names[cluster]         # For the other clusters
  ))

# Assign shape 'triangle' for 'Landrace', 'Improved', 'Wild sorghum' in both datasets
combined_data <- bind_rows(data, data2)

combined_data <- combined_data %>%
  mutate(shape = case_when(
    ClusterName %in% c("Improved", "Wild sorghum", "Landrace") ~ "triangle",  # Assign triangle for specific clusters
    TRUE ~ "circle"  # Assign circle for other clusters
  ))

# Handle NA values in ClusterName column
combined_data <- combined_data %>%
  filter(!is.na(ClusterName))  # Remove rows with NA clusters

# Get world map data
world <- map_data("world")
centroids <- world %>%
  group_by(region) %>%
  summarise(long = mean(range(long)), lat = mean(range(lat)))

# Merge data with map centroids
map_df <- combined_data %>%
  left_join(centroids, by = c("Country" = "region")) %>%
  filter(!is.na(long))  # Keep only countries that matched map data

# Define color palette for clusters
cluster_colors <- c("durra" = "green", "kafir" = "orange", "caudatum" = "purple", 
                    "milo/durra-bicolor" = "gold", "mixed/bicolor" = "brown", "guinea" = "red", 
                    "Improved" = "lightpink", "Wild sorghum" = "navy", "Landrace" = "blue")

# Plot the combined map with customized legend for shapes
p = ggplot(world, aes(long, lat)) +
  geom_polygon(aes(group = group), fill = "white", color = "gray70") +
  geom_point(data = map_df, aes(x = long, y = lat, color = ClusterName, shape = shape), alpha = 0.8, size = 3) +
  scale_color_manual(values = cluster_colors) +
  scale_shape_manual(values = c("circle" = 16, "triangle" = 17), 
                     labels = c("circle" = "SAP", "triangle" = "Sorgsd")) +  # Change legend labels for shapes
  theme_minimal() +
  labs(x = "Longitude", y = "Latitude", size = "Accessions", color = "Cluster", shape = "Plot") +
  theme(legend.position = "right")

ggsave("/Users/subhashmahamkali/Documents/gwas_sap/graphs/1.phenotype_distribution/geo_dist.pdf", 
       plot = p, device = "pdf", width = 9, height = 5, units = "in", 
       dpi = 500,
       useDingbats = FALSE)  

```




```{r}
install.packages(c("terra", "tidyverse", "raster", "sf", "rnaturalearth", "rnaturalearthdata"))

library(terra)           # for reading GeoTIFF
library(tidyverse)
library(sf)              # for spatial operations
library(rnaturalearth)   # for country boundaries
library(rnaturalearthdata)


# Read the nitrogen application raster (GeoTIFF)
n_raster <- rast("/Users/subhashmahamkali/Downloads/FertilizerCropSpecific_Geotiff/Fertilizer_sorghum/sorghum_NitrogenApplication_Rate.tif")  # rename if needed
plot(n_raster)  # quick look
# Get country polygons
# Step 1: Join nitrogen application data into the world_sf object

# Get country polygons
world_sf <- ne_countries(scale = "medium", returnclass = "sf")

# Calculate mean nitrogen application per country (ignoring NA values)
country_n_data <- terra::extract(n_raster, vect(world_sf), fun = mean, na.rm = TRUE)

# Combine with country names
country_n_data$Country <- world_sf$name
colnames(country_n_data)[2] <- "mean_N_app_kg_ha"

# Clean result
country_n_data <- country_n_data %>%
  dplyr::select(Country, mean_N_app_kg_ha) %>%
  mutate(N_Limited = mean_N_app_kg_ha < 50)  # e.g., <50 kg/ha threshold


world_sf <- world_sf %>%
  left_join(country_n_data, by = c("name" = "Country"))

# Step 2: Now plot with geom_sf() using the added 'mean_N_app_kg_ha' column
k = ggplot(world_sf) +
  geom_sf(aes(fill = mean_N_app_kg_ha), color = "gray80") +
  scale_fill_gradientn(colors = c("lightyellow", "orange", "darkred"), 
                       name = "N (kg/ha)", na.value = "gray95") +
  geom_point(data = map_df, aes(x = long, y = lat, color = ClusterName, shape = shape), 
             size = 3, alpha = 0.9) +
  scale_color_manual(values = cluster_colors) +
  scale_shape_manual(values = c("circle" = 16, "triangle" = 17)) +
  theme_minimal() +
  labs(title = "Geographical Distribution of Sorghum Accessions with N Application Background",
       x = "Longitude", y = "Latitude", color = "Cluster", shape = "Panel")

```

```{r}
map_df <- map_df %>%
  mutate(shape = case_when(
    ClusterName %in% c("Landrace", "Improved", "Wild sorghum") ~ ClusterName,
    TRUE ~ "Other"
  ))

# Define unique shapes for each special category
shape_values <- c("Landrace" = 17,       # triangle
                  "Improved" = 15,       # filled square
                  "Wild sorghum" = 18,   # diamond
                  "Other" = 16)          # filled circle (default SAP)


k = ggplot(world_sf) +
  geom_sf(aes(fill = mean_N_app_kg_ha), color = "gray80") +
  scale_fill_gradientn(colors = c("lightyellow", "orange", "darkred"), 
                       name = "N (kg/ha)", na.value = "gray95") +
  geom_point(data = map_df, aes(x = long, y = lat, color = ClusterName, shape = shape), 
             size = 3, alpha = 0.9) +
  scale_color_manual(values = cluster_colors) +
  scale_shape_manual(values = shape_values,
                     labels = c("Landrace" = "Landrace", 
                                "Improved" = "Improved", 
                                "Wild sorghum" = "Wild", 
                                "Other" = "SAP")) +
  theme_minimal() +
  labs(title = "Geographical Distribution of Sorghum Accessions with N Application Background",
       x = "Longitude", y = "Latitude", color = "Cluster", shape = "Type")


ggsave("/Users/subhashmahamkali/Documents/gwas_sap/graphs/1.phenotype_distribution/geo_dist_withN.pdf", 
       plot = k, device = "pdf", width = 9, height = 5, units = "in", 
       dpi = 500,
       useDingbats = FALSE)




# Keep only the subpopulations (exclude Landrace, Improved, Wild sorghum)
cluster_colors <- c(
  "durra" = "green",
  "kafir" = "orange",
  "caudatum" = "purple",
  "milo/durra-bicolor" = "gold",
  "mixed/bicolor" = "brown",
  "guinea" = "red"
)


map_df <- map_df %>%
  mutate(ClusterName = ifelse(ClusterName %in% c("Landrace", "Improved", "Wild sorghum"), NA, ClusterName))
k = ggplot(world_sf) +
  geom_sf(aes(fill = mean_N_app_kg_ha), color = "gray80") +
  scale_fill_gradientn(colors = c("lightyellow", "orange", "darkred"), 
                       name = "N (kg/ha)", na.value = "gray95") +
  geom_point(data = map_df, aes(x = long, y = lat, color = ClusterName, shape = shape), 
             size = 3, alpha = 0.9, na.rm = TRUE) +
  scale_color_manual(values = cluster_colors, na.translate = FALSE) +  # remove NA colors
  scale_shape_manual(values = shape_values,
                     labels = c("Landrace" = "Landrace", 
                                "Improved" = "Improved", 
                                "Wild sorghum" = "Wild", 
                                "Other" = "SAP")) +
  theme_minimal() +
  labs(title = "Geographical Distribution of Sorghum Accessions with N Application Background",
       x = "Longitude", y = "Latitude", color = "Cluster", shape = "Type")


```



```{r, eval=FALSE}
@article{mueller2012closing,
  title={Closing yield gaps through nutrient and water management},
  author={Mueller, Nathaniel D and Gerber, James S and Johnston, Matt and Ray, Deepak K and Ramankutty, Navin and Foley, Jonathan A},
  journal={Nature},
  volume={490},
  number={7419},
  pages={254--257},
  year={2012},
  publisher={Nature Publishing Group}
}



@misc{earthstat_nutrient,
  title = {Nutrient Application for Major Crops},
  author = {{EarthStat.org}},
  year = {2024},
  url = {https://www.earthstat.org/nutrient-application},
  note = {Accessed May 2025}
}

```
